title: Ghost of a Tale

gpu: NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1060 with Max-Q Design
notes:
You need first Protontricks:
https://github.com/Matoking/protontricks

The i open the Terminal an looked for the game :


protontricks -s Ghost of a tale


something like this should come up :


Found the following games:
Ghost of a Tale (417290)


now you open the Gui:

protontricks 417290 --gui

Select the Default Wineprefix

Then:

Install a Windows DLL 

Install here : dotne40 and vcrun2010

After installed start the game with 

"PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1  DefaultLimitNOFILE=1048576 DXVK_HUD=1 %command%"

and Your done :)

A good Video who helped me was :

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=zUJz-SdWul4
title: Ghost of a Tale

notes: Biggest problem is your mouse losses focus from the game window and no controller support.
title: Ghost of a Tale

notes:
Game runs smoothly at near windows performance after disabling almost all postprocessing. Motion blur incurs the highest amount of performance penalty, and disabling it gives 60%+ performance, so it's highly recommended you disable that.

The game still does not work with a controller.
title: Ghost of a Tale

notes: I played just a little, but the game ran perfectly so far. I only had to disable the post-processing effects (especially motion blur) to gain Windows-near performance. Also, my Xbox 360 controller works perfectly in the game.
title: Ghost of a Tale

notes:
Only the drivers 384 works, don't start with 410+.
Regularly black/dark (ambient light only) 3d scene, or crash (error popup), which requires a restarting, otherwise normally playable.
title: Ghost of a Tale

notes:
The game runs about the same (if perhaps a bit better) as on Proton 4.9-x. There is some minor hanging in the forest area though, an issue that wasn't apparent in Proton 4.9-x to the best of my memory. As with the last Proton version, the game still faces the same problems with postprocessing, especially motion blur. Bloom, however, seems to have a negligible impact on performance. 

Given this, it's still recommended to have almost all postprocessing off except for bloom and perhaps anti-aliasing (anti-aliasing seems to incur a penalty of about 5-10%) for those who would prefer to keep that on.

I tested the game again with two different controllers: Playstation Dualshock 4 and Switch Pro Controller. I'm happy to report that the Dualshock controller now works flawlessly. However, the game is still sadly unplayable with the Switch Pro controller, my preferred controller, having the same issues as with others' past reports.
title: Ghost of a Tale
